items.text: Heads up, hungry friends! The Maple Street food truck is parked outside Foster Library today from 11 AM to 2 PM. Get the chicken taco special for just $3 — limit two per student ID. Card or cash. Don't miss it!

questions.stem: According to the post, what is the price of the special?
questions.explanation: The post says 'Get the chicken taco special for just $3.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a student who wants four tacos?

options.A: The student must bring a faculty ID card
options.B: The student should pay with cash only
options.C: The student will need to skip the line
options.D: The student would need a second student to help

questions.explanation: The post limits the special to two per student ID, so four would require a second student's purchase.

items.text: Hi Anika, Your unused meal plan balance of $42.75 will be refunded to your student account by June 5. No action is required from you. Refunds appear under 'Dining Credits' in the student portal. Best, Dining Services

questions.stem: According to the email, by what date will the refund be processed?
questions.explanation: The email states the refund will appear 'by June 5.'

questions.stem: Where in the portal will the refund appear?
questions.explanation: The email says refunds appear under 'Dining Credits' in the student portal.

items.text: Dear Mateo, Your campus parking permit is ready for pickup at the Transportation Office, Room 110, on weekdays from 9 AM to 4 PM. Please bring a valid student ID. Permits not collected by May 30 will be voided. Regards, Ms. Ortega

questions.stem: According to the email, where should the student go to collect the permit?

options.C: The Parking Services kiosk near Lot D
options.D: The Ms. Ortega office in Library Hall

questions.explanation: The email states the pickup location is 'the Transportation Office, Room 110.'

questions.stem: According to the email, what happens if the permit is not collected by May 30?

options.A: A late fee of $10 will apply
options.B: A new request form must be filed
options.C: An email reminder will be sent
options.D: The unused permit will be voided

questions.explanation: The email says permits not collected by May 30 'will be voided.'

items.text: Unclaimed Lost-and-Found items held at the Anderson Hall front desk will be disposed of after June 12. Owners must present a valid ID and pay a $5 handling fee. Donation will follow for any remaining electronics and clothing.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is required to reclaim an item?

options.B: Original receipt and a written request
options.D: Student email and a parking permit

questions.explanation: The notice states owners 'must present a valid ID and pay a $5 handling fee.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about an unclaimed laptop?

options.A: It will be moved to the Transportation Office
options.B: It will be sold at a campus auction
options.C: It will be donated after the deadline
options.D: It will be stored for an additional month

questions.explanation: The notice notes donation will follow for remaining electronics, implying the laptop would be donated.
